✅ VERA — FDA APPROVED · HOLDING

FDA granted accelerated approval for Trutakna (atacicept-vymj) on Tuesday July 7, 2026 for IgA Nephropathy.
Est. price ~$41.23 — slightly below entry $41.7755. Normal post-catalyst drift after approval spike. Thesis intact.
14 analysts · Strong Buy · avg target $78.

▸ Next binary: ORIGIN 3 confirmatory eGFR endpoint — Q3 2026

Accelerated approval is conditional on ORIGIN 3. If eGFR endpoint disappoints, FDA can withdraw. Phase 3 proteinuria data (−46% vs placebo) is strong. Stop at $38 (pre-PDUFA floor) gives you room for normal volatility. Don’t sell below $55 — that’s still only 1.3× entry with $78 analyst target outstanding.

Stop (pre-PDUFA floor):$38.00   ~$3.23 (7.8%) below est. price — safe
TP1 — sell 4 shares at:$55.00 · post-approval stabilization zone

This weekend’s action item: Screen REPL on Musaffa. If clean, flag me and I’ll size the entry.

Your call.

📊 IBKR Intelligence

Account performance (TWR/MWR), portfolio allocation, investment themes, and peer data unavailable — IBKR connector offline this run. Will populate on next successful IBKR connection.

📚 Day 10 — Share-Based Compensation (SBC)

When you see a tech company report “non-GAAP EPS $5.00” and “GAAP EPS $3.50”, the $1.50 gap is mostly Share-Based Compensation. SBC is real economic dilution — the company is paying its employees with new shares, which shrinks your ownership percentage. It just doesn’t show up as a cash outflow.

Why management strips it out (and why you shouldn’t blindly follow): Companies argue SBC is a non-cash charge and doesn’t affect operations. That’s partially true. But every RSU and option that vests is a claim on the business. When ANET reports ~$48M SBC per quarter on ~$337M diluted shares outstanding, that’s about 0.5% annualized dilution — compounding quietly every year. Buybacks are supposed to offset it, but many companies buy back fewer shares than they issue.

The number to watch — Net Buyback Yield: Gross buybacks − SBC as % of market cap. If a company buys back $2B of stock but issues $1.8B in SBC, the net dilution reduction is only $200M. Companies that run negative net buyback yields are effectively paying employees with your equity.

How to use this in practice: When analyzing a tech stock, look at the GAAP vs non-GAAP spread on the income statement. A spread >15% deserves scrutiny. Check share count growth YoY — if diluted shares are growing faster than EPS, SBC is eating returns. ANET’s non-GAAP operating margin is ~47%; GAAP is ~37%. The 10-point spread is SBC + amortization. Both are real, not accounting noise.

Relevant today: ANET exit at $185 locked in +$31.56 net — that’s after exit commission. GAAP P&L on your trade was the gross. The “commission eats profit” rule in your playbook is the same concept applied to your own trades: always net the friction.

Research only — not financial advice. All decisions are Abbas Al Madani’s own.
Prices: web-estimated (IBKR offline). ANET: ~$186.96 Fri Jul 10 close (web). VERA: ~$41.23 Fri Jul 10 est. (web). Neither verified by IBKR.
